arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllah
translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: Whoever says SubhanAllah 100 times, his sins are wiped even if like the foam of the sea.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Alhamdulillah
translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Alhamdulillah fills the scales of good deeds.

arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
transliteration: Allahu Akbar
translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar — more beloved than all that is under the sun.

arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ
transliteration: La ilaha illallah
translation: There is no god but Allah
reward: Whoever says it 100 times: reward of freeing 10 slaves, 100 good deeds written, 100 sins erased, protected from Shaytan.

arabic: لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
transliteration: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
translation: There is no power nor strength except with Allah
reward: It is a treasure from the treasures of Paradise.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi
translation: Glory be to Allah and His is the praise
reward: Whoever says it 100 times morning and evening — comes on Judgment Day with nothing better except one who said more.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ
transliteration: SubhanAllahi al-Azeem
translation: Glory be to Allah, the Almighty
reward: Two phrases light on the tongue, heavy on the scales, beloved to the Most Merciful.

arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
transliteration: Astaghfirullah
translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
reward: The Prophet used to seek forgiveness 70+ times daily. Whoever does Istighfar, Allah relieves his distress and provides from where he cannot imagine.

arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
transliteration: Astaghfirullaha wa atubu ilayh
translation: I seek Allah's forgiveness and repent to Him
reward: The Prophet said: I seek forgiveness 70 times daily.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا صَنَعْتُ أَبُوءُ لَكَ بِنِعْمَتِكَ عَلَيَّ وَأَبُوءُ بِذَنْبِي فَاغْفِرْ لِي فَإِنَّهُ لَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
transliteration: Allahumma anta rabbi la ilaha illa anta, khalaqtani wa ana abduk, wa ana ala ahdika wa wa'dika mastata'tu, a'udhu bika min sharri ma sana'tu, abu'u laka bini'matika alayya, wa abu'u bidhanbI, faghfirli fa innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa ant
translation: O Allah, You are my Lord. None has the right to be worshipped but You. You created me and I am Your servant. I abide by Your covenant and Your promise as best I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge before You Your blessings upon me and I acknowledge my sins. So forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.
reward: The master of all prayers for forgiveness. Whoever says it with firm belief in the morning and dies that day will be among the people of Paradise.

arabic: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Asbahna wa asbahal mulku lillahi walhamdu lillah
translation: We have entered the morning and the dominion belongs to Allah, and all praise is for Allah
reward: Sunnah morning dhikr — brings barakah and protection for the day.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
transliteration: Allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaykan-nushur
translation: O Allah, by You we enter the morning, by You we enter the evening, by You we live and die, and to You is the resurrection
reward: Comprehensive morning supplication from the Sunnah.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لَا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلَا نَوْمٌ
transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyum, la ta'khudhuhu sinatun wa la nawm
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer. Neither drowsiness nor sleep overtakes Him
reward: Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi in the morning will be protected by Allah until evening.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi
translation: Glory be to Allah and His is the praise
reward: Whoever says it 100 times morning and evening — no one will come on Judgment Day with something better.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
transliteration: Allahumma inni as'alukal-afiyata fid-dunya wal-akhirah
translation: O Allah, I ask You for well-being in this world and the next
reward: No one is given a gift better than well-being.

arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
transliteration: Qul huwa Allahu ahad (3x each: Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, An-Nas)
translation: Say: He is Allah, the One — (recite Surahs Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, An-Nas)
reward: Whoever recites the three Quls 3 times morning and evening, they will suffice him against everything.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka ilman nafi'an wa rizqan tayyiban wa amalan mutaqabbala
translation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, good provision, and accepted deeds
reward: Recommended after Fajr prayer every morning.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ
transliteration: Allahumma salli ala Muhammad
translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad
reward: Whoever sends 100 prayers upon me in the morning — his needs are met and his sins forgiven.

arabic: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Amsayna wa amsal mulku lillahi walhamdu lillah
translation: We have entered the evening and the dominion belongs to Allah, and all praise is for Allah
reward: Evening counterpart of the morning Sunnah dhikr. Brings barakah through the night.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا وَبِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ
transliteration: Allahumma bika amsayna wa bika asbahna wa bika nahya wa bika namutu wa ilaykal-masir
translation: O Allah, by You we enter the evening, by You the morning, by You we live and die, and to You is the final return
reward: Complete evening supplication from the Sunnah.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi
translation: Glory be to Allah and His is the praise
reward: Said 100 times morning and evening — comes on Judgment Day with nothing better.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyum
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
reward: Reciting Ayat al-Kursi in the evening grants protection until morning.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
transliteration: A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created
reward: Whoever says this 3 times in the evening will not be harmed by any poison or bite that night.

arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
transliteration: Qul huwa Allahu ahad (3x each: Al-Ikhlas, Al-Falaq, An-Nas)
translation: Say: He is Allah, the One — recite the three Quls
reward: Reciting the three Quls 3 times in the evening suffices against everything.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyum
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
reward: Recite Ayat al-Kursi before sleep — a guardian from Allah will protect you, Shaytan will not come near until morning.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllah
translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: Before sleeping: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 34 Allahu Akbar — this is better for you than a servant.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Alhamdulillah
translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Part of the bedtime tasbih prescribed by the Prophet to Fatimah and Ali.

arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
transliteration: Allahu Akbar
translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: Part of the bedtime tasbih — better for you than having a servant.

arabic: بِسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا
transliteration: Bismika Allahumma amutu wa ahya
translation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live
reward: The Prophet used to say this every night before sleeping.

arabic: تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ
transliteration: Tabarakal-ladhi biyadihil-mulk
translation: Blessed is He in Whose hand is the dominion (Surah Al-Mulk)
reward: Surah Al-Mulk intercedes for its reciter and protects from the punishment of the grave.

arabic: قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ
transliteration: Qul huwa Allahu ahad (3x)
translation: Say: He is Allah, the One (Surah Al-Ikhlas — 3 times)
reward: Reciting Al-Ikhlas 3x before sleeping equals reciting the entire Quran.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ
transliteration: SubhanAllah
translation: Glory be to Allah
reward: After every obligatory prayer: 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 33 Allahu Akbar — sins forgiven even if like the foam of the sea.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Alhamdulillah
translation: All praise is for Allah
reward: Said 33 times after every obligatory prayer. Fills the scales on the Day of Judgment.

arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
transliteration: Allahu Akbar
translation: Allah is the Greatest
reward: Said 33 times after every obligatory prayer. Together with the tasbihat completes 100 remembrances.

arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
transliteration: Allahu la ilaha illa huwal-hayyul-qayyum
translation: Allah — there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
reward: Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every obligatory prayer is guaranteed Paradise when they die.

arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
transliteration: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamd, wa huwa ala kulli shay'in qadir
translation: None has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, no partner has He. His is the dominion and the praise, and He has power over all things
reward: Whoever says this after every obligatory prayer — his sins are forgiven even if they are like the foam of the sea.

arabic: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
transliteration: Astaghfirullah
translation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
reward: The Prophet used to say Astaghfirullah 3 times after every prayer.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
transliteration: Allahumma salli ala Muhammadin wa ala ali Muhammad, kama sallayta ala Ibrahima wa ala ali Ibrahim, innaka Hamidun Majid
translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, as You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. Indeed You are the Most Praiseworthy, Most Glorious
reward: Whoever sends one blessing upon the Prophet, Allah sends ten upon him.

arabic: صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ
transliteration: Sallallahu alayhi wa sallam
translation: May Allah's peace and blessings be upon him
reward: Allah sends ten blessings upon anyone who sends one upon the Prophet.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ
transliteration: Allahumma salli ala Muhammad (increase abundantly on Fridays)
translation: O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad — increase abundantly on Fridays
reward: The Prophet commanded us to increase prayers upon him on Friday — they are presented to him on that day.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ لَكَ الْحَمْدُ أَنْتَ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
transliteration: Allahumma lakal-hamd, anta nurus-samawati wal-ard
translation: O Allah, to You belongs all praise. You are the light of the heavens and the earth
reward: The Prophet used to begin Tahajjud with this powerful du'a.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ الْمَلِكِ الْقُدُّوسِ
transliteration: SubHanal-Malikil-Quddus
translation: Glory be to the Sovereign, the Most Holy
reward: Recited 3 times in Witr prayer, raising the voice on the third.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي فِيمَنْ هَدَيْتَ وَعَافِنِي فِيمَنْ عَافَيْتَ وَتَوَلَّنِي فِيمَنْ تَوَلَّيْتَ
transliteration: Allahumma-hdini fiman hadayt, wa afini fiman afayt, wa tawallani fiman tawallayt
translation: O Allah, guide me among those You have guided, grant me well-being among those You have granted well-being, and befriend me among those You have befriended
reward: Dua al-Qunut recited in Witr prayer.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ حَسَّنْتَ خَلْقِي فَحَسِّنْ خُلُقِي
transliteration: Allahumma anta hassanta khalqi fa-hassin khuluqi
translation: O Allah, just as You made my physical form beautiful, make my character beautiful too
reward: Dua when looking in the mirror.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
transliteration: A'udhu billahi minash-shaytanir-rajim
translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
reward: The Prophet said: If you are angry, seek refuge in Allah from Shaytan — anger will leave.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ
transliteration: Alhamdulillah (when sneezing) / Yarhamukallah (response)
translation: All praise is for Allah — the one who hears responds: May Allah have mercy on you
reward: When you sneeze, say Alhamdulillah. Those who hear say Yarhamukallah.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ الْمَوْلِجِ وَخَيْرَ الْمَخْرَجِ
transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka khayral-mawliji wa khayral-makhraji
translation: O Allah, I ask You for the best entrance and the best exit
reward: Du'a when entering the house.

arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina adhaban-nar
translation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and protect us from the punishment of the Fire
reward: The most comprehensive du'a — the Prophet used to say it often.

arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
transliteration: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zalimin
translation: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers
reward: The du'a of Prophet Yunus. No Muslim calls upon Allah with it except that Allah responds.

arabic: حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
transliteration: Hasbiyallahu la ilaha illa huwa, alayhi tawakkaltu wa huwa rabbul-arshil-azim
translation: Sufficient for me is Allah; there is no deity except Him. On Him I have relied, and He is the Lord of the Great Throne
reward: Whoever says this 7 times morning and evening, Allah suffices him.

arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
transliteration: Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa illam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakunanna minal-khassirin
translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves; and if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we will surely be among the losers
reward: The du'a of Prophet Adam and Hawwa — said when seeking forgiveness.

arabic: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً
transliteration: Rabbana la tuzigh qulubana ba'da idh hadaytana wa hab lana min ladunka rahmah
translation: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us and grant us from Yourself mercy
reward: Dua for steadfastness in faith — essential for the Ummah.

arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
transliteration: Rabbana afrigh alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdamana wansurna alal-qawmil-kafirin
translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience, plant firmly our feet, and give us victory over the disbelieving people
reward: Dua of the believers before battle — for the victory of the Ummah.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ انْصُرِ الْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
transliteration: Allahumma-nsur al-mustad'afina minal-mu'minin
translation: O Allah, grant victory to the weak and oppressed among the believers
reward: Dua for the oppressed Muslims globally — make it a daily habit.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّةِ مِنْ غَضَبِهِ وَعِقَابِهِ وَشَرِّ عِبَادِهِ وَمِنْ هَمَزَاتِ الشَّيَاطِينِ
transliteration: A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min ghadabihi wa iqabihi wa sharri ibadihi wa min hamazatish-shayatin
translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from His anger, punishment, the evil of His servants, and from the whispers of devils
reward: Said upon waking from a bad dream — then spit to the left 3 times and the dream will not harm you.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
transliteration: A'udhu billahi minash-shaytanir-rajim
translation: I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Shaytan
reward: When you see a bad dream: spit to your left 3 times, say this 3 times, and change your side.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
transliteration: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhin-nushur
translation: All praise is for Allah who gave us life after having taken it from us, and to Him is the resurrection
reward: The first words of the Prophet upon waking from sleep.

arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ
transliteration: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah
translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner
reward: Whoever says this 10 times upon waking has 10 good deeds written and 10 sins erased.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي كَسَانِي هَذَا وَرَزَقَنِيهِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَوْلٍ مِنِّي وَلَا قُوَّةٍ
transliteration: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi kasani hadha wa razaqanihi min ghayri hawlin minni wa la quwwah
translation: All praise is for Allah who has clothed me with this and provided it for me without any power from myself
reward: Whoever says this when putting on a garment has his past sins forgiven.

arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
transliteration: Bismillah
translation: In the name of Allah
reward: There is no wudu for the one who does not begin with the name of Allah.

arabic: أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَأَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّدًا عَبْدُهُ وَرَسُولُهُ
transliteration: Ash-hadu alla ilaha illallahu wa ash-hadu anna Muhammadan abduhu wa rasuluh
translation: I testify there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is His servant and messenger
reward: Whoever says this after wudu — the eight gates of Paradise open for him.

arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ
transliteration: Bismillah
translation: In the name of Allah
reward: When you eat, say Bismillah. If you forget at the start, say Bismillah in its beginning and end.

arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَطْعَمَنَا وَسَقَانَا وَجَعَلَنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
transliteration: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi at'amana wa saqana wa ja'alana muslimin
translation: All praise is for Allah who fed us, gave us drink, and made us Muslims
reward: Dua after eating.

arabic: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ وَلَجْنَا وَبِسْمِ اللَّهِ خَرَجْنَا وَعَلَى اللَّهِ رَبِّنَا تَوَكَّلْنَا
transliteration: Bismillahi walajna, wa bismillahi kharajna, wa ala Allahi rabbina tawakkalna
translation: In the name of Allah we enter, in the name of Allah we leave, and upon Allah our Lord we rely
reward: Say this when entering the home — Shaytan is turned away and cannot spend the night.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
transliteration: A'udhu bikalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
translation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created
reward: Whoever says this when arriving at a place — nothing will harm him until he leaves.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ فَإِنَّكَ تَقْدِرُ وَلَا أَقْدِرُ وَتَعْلَمُ وَلَا أَعْلَمُ وَأَنتَ عَلَّامُ الْغُيُوبِ
transliteration: Allahumma inni astakhiruka bi'ilmika, wa astaqdiruka bi-qudratika, wa as'aluka min fadlikal-azim, fa innaka taqdiru wa la aqdiru, wa ta'lamu wa la a'lamu, wa anta allamul-ghuyub
translation: O Allah, I seek Your guidance by Your knowledge, I seek ability by Your power, and I ask You of Your great favour — for You have power and I have none, You know and I do not know, and You are the Knower of the unseen
reward: Prayed after 2 rak'ahs when making a decision. Allah then facilitates what is best.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِي أَبْوَابَ رَحْمَتِكَ
transliteration: Allahumma-ftah li abwaba rahmatik
translation: O Allah, open for me the gates of Your mercy
reward: Said when entering the mosque.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ
transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka min fadlik
translation: O Allah, I ask You of Your bounty
reward: Said when leaving the mosque.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدَّعْوَةِ التَّامَّةِ وَالصَّلَاةِ الْقَائِمَةِ آتِ مُحَمَّدًا الْوَسِيلَةَ وَالْفَضِيلَةَ
transliteration: Allahumma rabba hadhihi-d-da'watit-tammah was-salatil-qa'imah, ati Muhammadan al-wasilata wal-fadilah
translation: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and the established prayer, grant Muhammad the highest rank and honour
reward: Whoever says this after the adhan will have the Prophet's intercession on Judgment Day.

arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
transliteration: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal-wakil
translation: Allah is sufficient for us and He is the best Disposer of affairs
reward: Said by Ibrahim when thrown in the fire. Said by the Prophet when told people had gathered against him.

arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
transliteration: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un
translation: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return
reward: Said in times of calamity — Allah's blessings and mercy descend upon the one who says it.

arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
transliteration: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zalimin
translation: There is no god but You, glory be to You, I have indeed been among the wrongdoers
reward: No Muslim calls upon Allah with it except that Allah responds.

arabic: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ السَّمِيعِ الْعَلِيمِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
transliteration: A'udhu billahis-sami'il-alim minash-shaytanir-rajim
translation: I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, All-Knowing, from the accursed Shaytan
reward: Said when feeling waswas or doubts in the heart.

arabic: قُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ
transliteration: Qul a'udhu birabbil-falaq (Al-Falaq & An-Nas 3x)
translation: Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of the daybreak (recite Al-Falaq and An-Nas 3 times)
reward: Recite Al-Falaq and An-Nas 3 times morning and evening — they suffice against all harm.

arabic: تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
transliteration: Tawakkaltu alallahi wa la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
translation: I put my trust in Allah, and there is no might or power except with Allah
reward: Whoever says this when leaving his house: it is said to him — you are guided, defended and protected.

arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنقَلِبُونَ
transliteration: Allahu akbar (3x). SubHanal-ladhi sakh-khara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila rabbina lamunqalibun
translation: Allah is the Greatest (3x). Glory be to Him who has made this subservient to us, and we were not capable of that, and indeed to our Lord we will return
reward: Said when mounting a vehicle or beginning a journey.

arabic: آيِبُونَ تَائِبُونَ عَابِدُونَ لِرَبِّنَا حَامِدُونَ
transliteration: Ayibuna, ta'ibuna, abiduna, li-rabbina hamidun
translation: Returning, repenting, worshipping, and praising our Lord
reward: Said after completing a journey — the Prophet would say this upon returning.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ خَيْرَ هَذِهِ السُّوقِ وَخَيْرَ مَا فِيهَا وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهَا وَشَرِّ مَا فِيهَا
transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka khayra hadhis-suq wa khayra ma fiha, wa a'udhu bika min sharriha wa sharri ma fiha
translation: O Allah, I ask You for the good of this marketplace and the good that is in it, and I seek refuge in You from its evil and the evil that is in it
reward: Whoever says this entering the marketplace — 1 million good deeds written, 1 million sins erased.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَلِّفْ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِنَا وَأَصْلِحْ ذَاتَ بَيْنِنَا
transliteration: Allahumma allif bayna qulubina wa aslih dhata baynina
translation: O Allah, unite our hearts and set right our affairs between us
reward: Du'a for harmony in family and marriage.

arabic: بَارَكَ اللَّهُ لَكَ وَبَارَكَ عَلَيْكَ وَجَمَعَ بَيْنَكُمَا فِي خَيْرٍ
transliteration: Barakallahu laka wa baraka alayka wa jama'a baynakuma fi khayr
translation: May Allah bless you and shower blessings upon you and join you together in goodness
reward: The Sunnah congratulation for a newlywed couple.

arabic: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ
transliteration: Assalamu alaykum wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh
translation: Peace be upon you and the mercy and blessings of Allah
reward: Spreading salaam is a means of entering Paradise. The full greeting earns 30 hasanat.

arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
transliteration: SubHanaka Allahumma wa bihamdika, ash-hadu alla ilaha illa anta, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk
translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise. I testify there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You
reward: Kaffarat al-majlis — expiation for any gathering. Said at the end of every sitting.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَيِّبًا نَافِعًا
transliteration: Allahumma sayyiban nafi'a
translation: O Allah, make it beneficial rain
reward: The Prophet would expose himself to rain saying this.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي يُسَبِّحُ الرَّعْدُ بِحَمْدِهِ
transliteration: SubHanal-ladhi yusabbihur-ra'du bihamdih
translation: Glory be to Him whom the thunder glorifies with His praise
reward: Said when thunder is heard.

arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لَهُ وَارْحَمْهُ وَعَافِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنْهُ
transliteration: Allahumma-ghfir lahu warhamhu wa afihi wa'fu anh
translation: O Allah, forgive him, have mercy on him, grant him well-being, and pardon him
reward: Janazah prayer dua — pray fervently for the deceased.

arabic: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
transliteration: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un
translation: Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him we will return
reward: Said upon the death of someone — reminds us all belongs to Allah.

arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ تَبَارَكَ اسْمُكَ وَتَعَالَى جَدُّكَ وَلَا إِلَهَ غَيْرُكَ
transliteration: SubHanaka Allahumma wa bihamdika tabarakas-muka wa ta'ala jadduka wa la ilaha ghayruk
translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise, blessed is Your name, exalted is Your majesty, and there is no god but You
reward: Opening supplication of gatherings.

arabic: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
transliteration: SubHanaka Allahumma wa bihamdika ash-hadu alla ilaha illa anta astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk
translation: Glory be to You O Allah and Your praise. I testify there is no god but You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You
reward: Expiation of the gathering — seals the session and forgives what was said.

arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
transliteration: Labbayk Allahumma labbayk, labbayk la sharika laka labbayk, innal-hamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk, la sharika lak
translation: Here I am O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner. Verily all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner
reward: The Talbiyah of Hajj and Umrah — said continuously from Ihram until Tawaf. Every stone and tree joins in.

arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
transliteration: SubhanAllah wal-hamdu lillah wa la ilaha illallah wallahu akbar
translation: Glory be to Allah, all praise is for Allah, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest
reward: Said during tawaf around the Ka'bah — the four phrases most beloved to Allah.
